questions.question: A sound wave has a frequency of $500\text{ Hz}$ and a wavelength of $0.68\text{ m}$. What is its speed?
questions.explanation: $v = f\lambda = 500 \times 0.68 = 340\text{ m/s}$ — the approximate speed of sound in air.

questions.question: A sound wave travels at $340\text{ m/s}$ with a wavelength of $1.7\text{ m}$. What is its frequency?
A sound wave travels at $340\text{ m/s}$ with a frequency of $850\text{ Hz}$. What is its wavelength?
Sound travels at approximately $1,500\text{ m/s}$ in water. A sonar signal has a frequency of $30\text{ kHz}$. What is its wavelength?
A sound wave has a period of $0.004\text{ s}$. What is its frequency?
As the temperature of air increases, the speed of sound in air:
questions.explanation: Warmer air has faster-moving particles, which transmit sound vibrations more quickly.

questions.question: In which medium does sound travel slowest?
questions.explanation: Sound travels slowest in gases, faster in liquids, and fastest in solids. Air is a gas.

questions.question: A thunderclap is heard $4.0\text{ s}$ after the lightning flash. If the speed of sound is $340\text{ m/s}$, how far away was the lightning?
A sound wave has frequency $680\text{ Hz}$ and speed $340\text{ m/s}$. What is its wavelength in cm?
A dog whistle produces sound at $22,500\text{ Hz}$. What is this frequency in kHz?
If the frequency of a sound wave is doubled while the speed remains constant, its wavelength:
questions.explanation: $v = f\lambda$. At constant $v$, $f$ and $\lambda$ are inversely proportional. Doubling $f$ halves $\lambda$.

questions.question: A sound wave in steel has a frequency of $2,000\text{ Hz}$ and a wavelength of $2.5\text{ m}$. What is the speed of sound in steel?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the speed of sound and the wave equation!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the relationship $v = f\lambda$ and how speed varies in different media.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on unit conversions (kHz, cm) and solving for each variable.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: $v = f\lambda$, speed of sound in air ($\approx 340\text{ m/s}$), and factors affecting sound speed.
